engineGuide.h2: Range Rover Sport Engine Codes - Full Technical Specifications & Replacement Guide
engineGuide.h3: Every engine code fitted to the Range Rover Sport across all generations, with technical specs, compatible variants and trims, known failure points, and average rebuilt prices from UK specialists. Select any engine code to compare quotes.

entries.title: 2.7L Diesel
entries.history: The 2.7-litre TDV6 was Ford and PSA’s joint-venture Lion V6, fitted to first-generation L320 Range Rover Sport from launch.
compatibleVariants: L320 TDV6, L320 HSE TDV6, L320 SE TDV6
commonFailures: Crankshaft failure (shared weakness across Lion V6 family)
Oil pump failure
Injector seal leaks causing carbon buildup
entries.cta: Get quotes for 276DT Range Rover Sport engine replacement

entries.title: 3.0L Diesel
entries.history: The upgraded 3.0-litre TDV6 replaced the 2.7 in 2010, delivering 258bhp and 600Nm, but retained the catastrophic crankshaft design flaw that defines L320 and L494 replacement demand.
compatibleVariants: L320 TDV6, L320 HSE TDV6, L494 TDV6, L494 SE TDV6
commonFailures: Crankshaft snapping - the #1 TDV6/SDV6 failure, often without warning between 80k-120k miles
Timing chain stretch - rattling on cold start, codes P0008, P0016
EGR cooler leaks - coolant loss, potential hydrolock
entries.cta: Get quotes for 306DT Range Rover Sport engine replacement

entries.title: 3.0L Diesel
entries.history: The higher-output SDV6 variant of the 3.0 Lion V6, producing 292-306bhp and 700Nm, fitted to top-spec L320 and L494 diesel models from 2010.
compatibleVariants: L320 SDV6, L320 HSE SDV6, L494 SDV6, L494 HSE SDV6, L494 Autobiography SDV6
commonFailures: Crankshaft failure - shares crankshaft design weakness with 306DT
Turbo actuator faults - loss of power, underboost codes (P0299)
DPF blockage - short journeys accelerate regeneration failure
entries.cta: Get quotes for 306PS Range Rover Sport engine replacement

entries.title: 4.4L Diesel
entries.history: The 4.4-litre SDV8 V8 diesel, assembled at Ford’s Mexican plant, is widely considered the most reliable engine in the Range Rover Sport line-up, fitted exclusively to L494 high-end variants.
compatibleVariants: L494 SDV8, L494 Autobiography SDV8, L494 Westminster SDV8
commonFailures: Turbo drain pipe defects (early engines) - oil starvation risk
Belt tensioner failure - drive belt breaks due to roller displacement
Heat exchanger and oil pump leaks - common after 80k miles
entries.cta: Get quotes for 448DT Range Rover Sport engine replacement

entries.title: 3.0L MHEV Diesel
entries.history: The Ingenium straight-six mild-hybrid diesel arrived with the L461 third-generation Range Rover Sport in 2022, offering D250, D300, and D350 outputs with 48V MHEV assistance for reduced emissions and improved efficiency.
compatibleVariants: L461 D250, L461 D300, L461 D350, L461 HSE D300, L461 Autobiography D350
commonFailures: DPF regeneration issues - short journeys cause frequent active regens
SCR/AdBlue system faults - NOx sensor failures reported
EGR valve carbon buildup - intake clogging on urban cycles
entries.cta: Get quotes for AJ300D Range Rover Sport engine replacement

entries.title: 5.0L Supercharged V8
entries.history: The 5.0-litre supercharged V8 powered the Range Rover Sport from 2014-2017, delivering 510bhp and 625Nm. It remains one of the most sought-after performance engines, but owners must budget for documented timing chain and oil consumption issues.
compatibleVariants: L494 V8 Supercharged, L494 HSE V8 Supercharged, L494 Westminster V8 Supercharged, L494 Autobiography V8 Supercharged
commonFailures: Timing chain tensioner failure - plastic tensioners degrade, causing chain rattle and potential valve/piston contact (60k-80k miles)
Oil consumption - burns 1L/1000 miles, leading to spun rod bearings if ignored
Supercharger seal leaks - nose seal fails, oil contaminates belts
entries.cta: Get quotes for AJ133 Range Rover Sport engine replacement

entries.title: 5.0L Supercharged V8 (SVR)
entries.history: The high-performance SVR variant of the AJ133, producing 567bhp and 700Nm, fitted exclusively to the L494 SVR from 2015-2022. The most powerful factory engine in the pre-L461 generation.
compatibleVariants: L494 SVR, L494 SVR Carbon Edition
commonFailures: Timing chain tensioner failure - shared weakness with AJ133, accelerated in high-output tune
Oil consumption - higher stress magnifies lubrication issues
Supercharger coupler wear - rattling from supercharger nose at idle
entries.cta: Get quotes for AJ133S Range Rover Sport engine replacement

entries.title: 2.0L Petrol (Si4)
entries.history: The 2.0-litre turbocharged four-cylinder Ingenium petrol, badged Si4, was introduced in the L494 for drivers prioritising urban efficiency over motorway torque.
compatibleVariants: L494 Si4, L494 S Si4, L494 SE Si4
commonFailures: Turbocharger bearing failure - whining noise, loss of boost pressure
High-pressure fuel pump (HPFP) failure - hard starting, misfires
Timing chain stretch - rattle on cold start, common above 60k miles
entries.cta: Get quotes for PT204 Range Rover Sport engine replacement

entries.title: 3.0L MHEV Petrol (P400)
entries.history: The Ingenium straight-six mild-hybrid petrol, producing 400bhp, features both a twin-scroll turbocharger and a 48V electric supercharger for instant response. Fitted to L494 (2019-2022) and L461 models.
compatibleVariants: L494 P400, L494 HSE P400, L461 P400, L461 Autobiography P400
commonFailures: Ignition coil failure - misfires, rough idle, codes P0300-P0306 (40k-80k miles)
Direct injector coking - carbon buildup on intake valves due to direct injection
Fuel injector deposits - lean misfires under load
entries.cta: Get quotes for AJ300 Range Rover Sport engine replacement

entries.title: 4.4L Twin-Turbo V8 (P530)
entries.history: The third-generation L461 Range Rover Sport adopted BMW’s N63 4.4-litre twin-turbo V8 for the P530 model, replacing the Ford-derived AJ133. This hot-vee design places turbochargers inside the cylinder banks.
compatibleVariants: L461 P530, L461 P530 Autobiography, L461 P530 First Edition
commonFailures: Oil consumption - BMW N63 hot-vee design known for higher oil usage
Turbo coolant line leaks - heat degradation in confined space
Valve stem seal wear - blue smoke on startup (typical 70k-90k miles)
entries.cta: Get quotes for N63B44 Range Rover Sport engine replacement

entries.title: 2.0L PHEV (P400e)
entries.history: The plug-in hybrid powertrain combines the 2.0-litre Ingenium four-cylinder petrol engine with a 105kW electric motor and 13.1kWh battery. Total system output is 404hp, with up to 40km electric-only range.
compatibleVariants: L494 P400e, L494 HSE P400e, L494 Autobiography P400e
commonFailures: High-voltage battery degradation - reduced electric range over time
Charging system faults - AC-DC converter failures reported
Engine running rough when battery depleted - reliance on combustion engine at low charge
entries.cta: Get quotes for AJ200P Range Rover Sport engine replacement

entries.title: 3.0L PHEV (P510e)
entries.history: The third-generation L461 plug-in hybrid combines the 3.0-litre Ingenium petrol straight-six with a 105kW electric motor, delivering 510hp and significantly improved electric range over the P400e.
compatibleVariants: L461 P510e, L461 P510e Autobiography, L461 P510e First Edition
commonFailures: Thermal management system faults - battery cooling issues
Electric drive unit bearing wear - whine from rear motor
Software integration glitches - occasional drive mode errors
entries.cta: Get quotes for AJ300E Range Rover Sport engine replacement

engineGuide.closing: Can't find your engine code? Enter your registration number above - we'll instantly identify the exact unit, its technical specifications, and live replacement prices from UK Range Rover Sport engine specialists. Every rebuilt engine comes with a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty.

commonProblems.h2: Range Rover Sport Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense
commonProblems.h3: The Range Rover Sport delivers formidable off-road capability and luxury refinement, but its engine range has well-documented weak points. The 3.0 TDV6/SDV6 (306DT/306PS) dominates replacement demand due to catastrophic crankshaft failure, while the 5.0 AJ133 supercharged V8 suffers timing chain tensioner degradation and oil consumption. Across all diesel variants, EGR cooler leaks, turbo actuator faults, and DPF issues drive repair decisions. Every rebuilt engine supplied through EnginesMarket includes upgraded internal components designed to address the original failure point - plus a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty. Below, we break down each major failure, what it costs to fix at a dealer versus a specialist, and when replacement becomes the only sensible option.

problems.h4: Range Rover Sport Crankshaft Failure - The 3.0 TDV6/SDV6 Silent Killer
problems.affectedModels: Range Rover Sport L320 TDV6, L320 SDV6, L494 TDV6, L494 SDV6 (2010-2019, 306DT & 306PS engines)
problems.rootCause: A narrow bearing design inherited from the 2.7 TDV6, combined with oil starvation or extended service intervals, leads to bearing spin followed by crankshaft fracture. Once the crank snaps, the engine seizes instantly - no rebuild is viable.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply of hot-tested reconditioned long engine with upgraded crankshaft and bearings, plus 10-14 hours labour
repairOptions.longevity: 12-month unlimited mileage warranty; upgraded components address original design flaw

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Low-mileage used engine from donor vehicle, fitted with new consumables
repairOptions.longevity: 3-6 month warranty only; no guarantee original crankshaft weakness is resolved

problems.recommendation: There is no preventative fix for crankshaft failure - when the crank snaps, replacement is the only option. We recommend a reconditioned engine with an upgraded crankshaft and heavy-duty bearings over a used unit. The additional £1,000-£1,500 buys a 12-month unlimited warranty and assurance the known design flaw has been addressed. On a Range Rover Sport worth £8,000-£15,000, a proper reconditioned engine is the financially sensible choice.
problems.cta: Compare reconditioned 306DT / 306PS engine prices for your Range Rover Sport

problems.group: Range Rover Sport Timing Chain Failure
problems.h4: Range Rover Sport Timing Chain Failure - AJ133 V8 Tensioner Degradation
problems.affectedModels: Range Rover Sport L494 V8 Supercharged, Autobiography V8 (2014-2017, AJ133 5.0L supercharged V8)
problems.typicalFailureMileage: 60,000 - 80,000 miles (tensioner rattle audible from cold start)
problems.rootCause: Plastic timing chain tensioners degrade and lose hydraulic pressure, allowing chain slack. If ignored, the chain can skip teeth on the camshaft sprockets, causing valves to contact pistons - catastrophic engine failure requiring full replacement.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replacement of timing chains, tensioners, guides, and VVT hardware before failure occurs
repairOptions.longevity: Viable only if caught early (audible rattle but no running issues); upgraded metal tensioners prevent recurrence

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply of reconditioned AJ133 with upgraded timing chains, metal tensioners, and resealed supercharger
repairOptions.longevity: 12-month unlimited warranty; recommended if chain has already jumped or engine has suffered valve damage

problems.recommendation: If you hear timing chain rattle on cold start but the engine runs normally, invest in the preventative chain replacement immediately - it will be the best £1,500-£2,000 you spend on this vehicle. If the engine has already jumped timing or developed a knock, a reconditioned unit with upgraded tensioners is the only safe path. Do not risk a used AJ133 - many carry the same tensioner weakness.
problems.cta: Get quotes for AJ133 timing chain replacement or reconditioned V8 engine

problems.h4: Range Rover Sport Oil Consumption - AJ133 V8's Lubrication Problem
problems.affectedModels: Range Rover Sport L494 V8 Supercharged, SVR (2014-2022, AJ133 & AJ133S 5.0L supercharged V8)
problems.rootCause: The AJ133 V8 burns oil at a rate of up to 1 litre per 1,000 miles due to cylinder bore glazing, valve stem seal wear, and PCV system design. If oil levels are not meticulously maintained, spun rod bearings and bottom-end failure follow.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Oil analysis, PCV valve replacement, switch to high-performance 5W-50 oil, regular level monitoring
repairOptions.longevity: Ongoing management; does not fix root cause but can extend engine life if oil never runs low

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Cylinder head overhaul with valve guide seals, bore honing, new piston rings
Complete reconditioned AJ133 with upgraded oil control components and resealed PCV system
repairOptions.longevity: 12-month warranty; the permanent fix for high-mileage V8s with advanced oil burning

problems.recommendation: On a high-mileage AJ133 (80k+ miles) already burning significant oil, a full reconditioned engine delivers better long-term value than a top-end rebuild. The £1,500-£2,000 premium over a rebuild buys upgraded components, a full warranty, and assurance that the bottom end - where catastrophic failure occurs - is fresh. Check your oil every 500 miles without fail.
problems.cta: Compare reconditioned AJ133 engine prices for your V8 Range Rover Sport

problems.group: Range Rover Sport EGR Cooler Leak
problems.h4: Range Rover Sport EGR Cooler Leak - Diesel Hydrolock Risk
problems.affectedModels: Range Rover Sport 3.0 TDV6/SDV6 (2010-2019, L320 & L494 diesel variants)
problems.rootCause: The EGR cooler develops internal cracks, allowing coolant to leak into the exhaust gas recirculation system. Coolant can enter the intake manifold and, in severe cases, cause hydrolock - where liquid fills a cylinder and stops the piston from completing its stroke, bending connecting rods.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replacement of failed EGR cooler with OEM or upgraded unit; coolant flush
repairOptions.longevity: Viable if caught early (coolant loss but no engine running issues)

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Cooler replacement plus walnut blasting of intake manifold carbon deposits
repairOptions.longevity: Restores airflow and prevents future buildup-related failures

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Complete engine replacement if hydrolock has bent rods or caused crankshaft damage
repairOptions.longevity: Required if engine has suffered hydrolock - do not attempt repair

problems.recommendation: If you notice coolant loss without external leaks, or white smoke from the exhaust, have the EGR cooler inspected immediately. Replacement at specialist rates (£500-£900) is a fraction of the cost of a hydrolocked engine. On vehicles over 100k miles, combine cooler replacement with an intake decoke - the labour overlaps, and carbon removal improves performance and fuel economy.
problems.cta: Get EGR cooler replacement quotes for your Range Rover Sport diesel

problems.group: Range Rover Sport Turbo Actuator Failure
problems.h4: Range Rover Sport Turbo Actuator Failure - Boost Loss & Black Smoke
problems.rootCause: The electronic boost pressure actuator or its wiring harness corrodes and fails, preventing the variable geometry turbo from adjusting vanes. Symptoms include black smoke on acceleration, loss of power, whistling or hissing noises, and jerking under load . A damaged wiring harness leading to the actuator is a common finding .

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Repair or replacement of damaged wiring harness sections to the boost actuator
repairOptions.longevity: Suitable if actuator is still functional and only wiring corroded

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replacement of boost pressure actuator unit, typically with harness repair
repairOptions.longevity: Resolves actuator-specific failure codes; restores full boost control

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Complete turbocharger replacement if actuator failure has damaged turbine or caused overspeed
repairOptions.longevity: Required if turbo has suffered physical damage from prolonged actuator failure

problems.recommendation: Actuator and wiring issues typically present gradually - you'll notice intermittent power loss before complete failure. A specialist can diagnose with live data and often repair just the harness or actuator for under £500. Do not ignore early symptoms; a failing actuator can overspeed the turbo and turn a £500 repair into a £2,000 turbo replacement.
problems.cta: Compare turbo actuator repair prices for your Range Rover Sport diesel

problems.h4: Range Rover Sport DPF Blockage - Diesel Regeneration Failure
problems.affectedModels: Range Rover Sport 3.0 TDV6/SDV6 (2010-2019), 2.0 Ingenium diesel (2017-2022)
problems.rootCause: The diesel particulate filter requires regular passive regeneration - sustained motorway speeds to burn off soot. Vehicles used primarily for short urban journeys never complete regen cycles, leading to progressive blockage, back-pressure buildup, and eventual engine management warnings.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Diagnostic tool initiation of stationary regen to burn off accumulated soot
repairOptions.longevity: Works only if DPF is partially blocked (not fully clogged)

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Chemical or ultrasonic DPF cleaning while on or off vehicle
repairOptions.longevity: Restores filter function at 50-70% of replacement cost

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: New or professionally reconditioned DPF fitted with pressure sensor calibration
repairOptions.longevity: Permanent fix; required if filter is cracked or thermally damaged

problems.recommendation: If you see a DPF warning light, take the car for a 40-minute motorway run at 2,500-3,000 RPM first - it may clear. If the light returns, pay for a forced regen (£80-£150) before considering replacement. For urban-only drivers, switching to a petrol or hybrid vehicle may be more cost-effective than repeated DPF issues. A professional clean at £250-£450 is a solid mid-tier option for otherwise healthy vehicles.
problems.cta: Get DPF cleaning and replacement quotes for your Range Rover Sport diesel

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Range Rover Sport
finalCta.paragraph: Across six major failure types - from the 3.0 diesel's crankshaft weakness to the AJ133 V8's timing chain and oil consumption issues - the Range Rover Sport demands vigilant maintenance and honest decision-making when failure strikes. The good news is that specialist reconditioned engines with upgraded components are available at 40-60% below dealer pricing, each carrying a 12-month unlimited mileage warranty. EnginesMarket connects you with trusted UK Range Rover Sport specialists who supply and fit nationwide. Get quotes today, compare transparent prices, and get back on the road without the main dealer premium.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare Range Rover Sport Engine Replacement Prices Now →
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EnginesMarket's UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Range Rover Sport and engine code. Every engine supplied through EnginesMarket carries a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

engineSizes.h2: Range Rover Sport Engines by Size - Find the Right Displacement for Your Variant
engineSizes.intro: Every displacement fitted to the Range Rover Sport across all UK generations is listed below, with compatible engine codes, years, variants, and common failure notes.

groups.title: Range Rover Sport Diesel Engine Sizes

items.title: Range Rover Sport 2.0L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 2.0-litre Ingenium diesel joined the L494 Range Rover Sport in 2016 as the entry-level diesel option, prioritising fuel economy and lower CO₂ emissions over outright performance. Codenamed AJ200D, this four-cylinder turbo diesel was also offered in D180 and D240 power tunes before being phased out in favour of the straight-six MHEV units on the L461 generation.
items.cta: Get quotes for Range Rover Sport 2.0L diesel engines

items.title: Range Rover Sport 2.7L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 2.7-litre TDV6 was the original diesel powerplant for the first-generation L320 Range Rover Sport from launch in 2005 until 2013. A Ford Lion V6 shared with Land Rover Discovery 3 and Jaguar S-Type, it delivered respectable torque for towing but introduced the crankshaft weakness that would plague its larger 3.0-litre successor.
items.cta: Get quotes for Range Rover Sport 2.7L diesel engines

items.title: Range Rover Sport 3.0L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 3.0-litre V6 diesel became the backbone of the Range Rover Sport diesel range from 2010 onwards, replacing the 2.7 TDV6 and continuing through both L320 and L494 generations. Available in TDV6 (258hp) and SDV6 (292-306hp) tunes, this Ford Lion unit delivers strong towing ability and motorway refinement - but carries the notorious crankshaft snapping fault that dominates UK engine replacement demand.
commonFailurePoints: EGR cooler leaks causing hydrolock risk
items.cta: Get quotes for Range Rover Sport 3.0L diesel engines

items.title: Range Rover Sport 4.4L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 4.4-litre SDV8 V8 diesel arrived with the L494 generation in 2014 and remained available until 2020. A Ford Lion V8 unit developing 339bhp and 740Nm of torque, it offers effortless refinement and genuine V8 character in a diesel. Widely considered the most reliable engine in the Sport line-up, with fewer catastrophic failure reports than the V6 diesel units.
items.cta: Get quotes for Range Rover Sport 4.4L diesel engines

groups.title: Range Rover Sport Petrol Engine Sizes

items.title: Range Rover Sport 2.0L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 2.0-litre Si4 petrol joined the L494 Range Rover Sport in 2017 as the entry-level petrol option, using Jaguar Land Rover’s Ingenium four-cylinder turbocharged unit. Developing 300hp, it offered surprising punch for its size but never matched diesel sales volumes in the UK market.
items.cta: Compare Range Rover Sport 2.0L petrol engine prices

items.title: Range Rover Sport 3.0L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 3.0-litre petrol option arrived with the L461 third-generation Sport in 2019, using Jaguar Land Rover’s Ingenium straight-six architecture. Badged P400, it features both a twin-scroll turbocharger and a 48V electric supercharger for immediate throttle response, delivering 400hp with mild-hybrid assistance.
items.cta: Compare Range Rover Sport 3.0L petrol engine prices

items.title: Range Rover Sport 5.0L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 5.0-litre supercharged V8 is the definitive performance engine for the L494 Range Rover Sport. Codenamed AJ133, it delivered 510hp in standard V8 Supercharged form and 567hp in the SVR flagship. Fitted from 2014 to 2022, this Ford-derived AJ-V8 unit provides savage acceleration and a thunderous soundtrack - but owners must budget for documented timing chain tensioner and oil consumption issues.
commonFailurePoints: Plastic timing chain tensioner failure (60k-80k miles)
items.cta: Compare Range Rover Sport 5.0L petrol engine prices

groups.title: Range Rover Sport Hybrid / MHEV Engine Sizes

items.title: Range Rover Sport 2.0L Plug-in Hybrid Engines
items.description: The P400e plug-in hybrid joined the L494 Range Rover Sport in 2017, combining a 2.0-litre Ingenium four-cylinder petrol engine with a 105kW electric motor and 13.1kWh battery. Total system output is 404hp, with electric-only range of approximately 40km. The PHEV offered lower BIK tax for company car drivers before the L461 generation introduced larger battery options.
commonFailurePoints: engine running rough when battery depleted
items.cta: Enquire about Range Rover Sport 2.0L plug-in hybrid engine availability

items.title: Range Rover Sport 3.0L Mild-Hybrid Petrol Engines
items.description: The 3.0-litre P400 mild-hybrid petrol (detailed above) combines the Ingenium straight-six with a 48V Belt-integrated Starter Generator (BiSG) for energy recovery, stop-start refinement, and torque assist. This MHEV system improves fuel economy without requiring external charging.
items.cta: Enquire about Range Rover Sport 3.0L mild-hybrid engine availability

items.title: Range Rover Sport 3.0L Mild-Hybrid Diesel Engines
items.description: The third-generation L461 Range Rover Sport introduced the D250, D300, and D350 mild-hybrid diesel engines in 2022. Using the Ingenium AJ300D straight-six with 48V MHEV technology, these units deliver improved efficiency and reduced emissions while maintaining Land Rover’s legendary towing capability.
commonFailurePoints: DPF regeneration issues on short journeys
items.cta: Enquire about Range Rover Sport 3.0L mild-hybrid diesel engine availability

items.title: Range Rover Sport 3.0L Plug-in Hybrid Engines
items.description: The P510e is the flagship plug-in hybrid for the L461 generation, pairing a 3.0-litre Ingenium petrol straight-six with a 105kW electric motor and larger battery pack than the P400e. Total system output is 510hp, with significantly improved electric-only range - making it the most powerful PHEV in the Range Rover Sport line-up.
items.cta: Enquire about Range Rover Sport 3.0L plug-in hybrid engine availability

items.title: Range Rover Sport 4.4L Twin-Turbo Petrol Engines
items.description: The P530, introduced with the L461 third-generation Range Rover Sport in 2022, uses BMW’s N63 4.4-litre twin-turbo V8. This hot-vee design places the turbochargers inside the cylinder banks for improved response, delivering 530hp and 750Nm of torque. It replaces the Ford-derived AJ133 as the flagship performance engine.
items.cta: Enquire about Range Rover Sport 4.4L twin-turbo petrol engine availability

items.description: Diesel powered the majority of UK Range Rover Sports across L320, L494 and L461 generations - and the 3.0 TDV6/SDV6 remains the single most common engine replacement enquiry due to its well-documented crankshaft weakness.

items.title: Range Rover Sport Plug-in Hybrid (PHEV) Engines
items.description: Plug-in hybrid variants arrived with the L494 P400e in 2017 and continued with the more powerful L461 P510e - combining petrol engines with electric motors for lower company car tax and urban zero-emission driving.
items.descriptor: Plug-in hybrid variants arrived with the L494 P400e in 2017 and continued with the more powerful L461 P510e - combining petrol engines with electric motors for lower company car tax and urban zero-emission driving.

AJ300E - 3.0 Ingenium straight-six + 105kW e-motor (P510e, L461)

Electric torque assistance under acceleration reduces turbo lag

engineYears.h2: Range Rover Sport Engines by Year - What Was Fitted & When
engineYears.intro: This section covers every major engine change across the Range Rover Sport's production timeline from 2005 to the present - organised so owners can quickly identify their engine by year and compare rebuilt prices from UK specialists.

years.preview: L320 Launch: Ford Lion V6 Diesel & V8 Petrol
years.description: The first-generation Range Rover Sport (L320) launched alongside the Discovery 3, sharing its Ford-derived engine range - from the entry-level 2.7 TDV6 to the range-topping 4.2 supercharged V8.
keyChanges: 2005: L320 platform launched with 2.7 TDV6 diesel and 4.4 naturally aspirated V8
2006: 4.2 supercharged V8 introduced as performance flagship (390hp)
2008: Facelift with revised interior and minor engine refinement updates
popularModels: Range Rover Sport TDV6 · Range Rover Sport V8 · Range Rover Sport Supercharged
knownFor: The original Sport shape that defined the segment - muscular, road-focused, less off-road capable than full-size Range Rover
2.7 TDV6 suffers oil pump and injector seal failures but fewer crankshaft issues than later 3.0
4.2 supercharged V8 is rare; timing chain and supercharger coupler wear are known pain points
Early L320s now well into higher mileage; many diesel examples have already had one replacement engine
years.cta: Get quotes for 2005-2009 Range Rover Sport replacement engines
years.ticker: Range Rover Sport 2.7 TDV6 engine replacement • Range Rover Sport 4.2 supercharged V8 recon • L320 crankshaft failure
enquiries: Range Rover Sport 2.7 TDV6 engine replacement
Range Rover Sport 4.2 supercharged V8 recon

years.description: The 2010 facelift brought the 3.0-litre TDV6 and SDV6 diesel engines to the L320 - and with them, the catastrophic crankshaft snapping issue that would define Sport replacement demand for the next decade.
keyChanges: 2010: 2.7 TDV6 replaced by 3.0 TDV6 (258hp) and 3.0 SDV6 (292hp)
2010: 5.0 naturally aspirated V8 replaces 4.4; 5.0 supercharged V8 replaces 4.2
2011: 8-speed automatic transmission introduced across diesel range
2012: Minor styling updates and improved infotainment
popularModels: Range Rover Sport TDV6 · Range Rover Sport SDV6 · Range Rover Sport V8 Supercharged · Range Rover Sport Autobiography
knownFor: The 3.0 TDV6/SDV6 crankshaft snapping problem - the single biggest engine failure driver across all Sport generations
5.0 supercharged AJ133 offers brutal performance but carries timing chain tensioner and oil consumption risks
8-speed ZF transmission significantly improves refinement over earlier 6-speed units
L320 facelift models remain popular second-hand buys - but engine history is critical
years.cta: Compare 2010-2013 Range Rover Sport engine prices
years.ticker: Range Rover Sport 3.0 TDV6 engine replacement • Range Rover Sport 306DT crankshaft snapped • SDV6 recon engine price • 5.0 supercharged V8 timing chain failure
enquiries: Range Rover Sport 3.0 TDV6 engine replacement

years.preview: L494 Launch: SDV8 Arrives, Supercharged V8 Matures
years.description: The second-generation L494 Range Rover Sport launched in 2013 on an all-new aluminium architecture - lighter, sharper, and introducing the 4.4 SDV8 diesel while carrying over the 3.0 V6 diesel and 5.0 supercharged V8.
keyChanges: 2013: All-new L494 platform (aluminium construction, 420kg lighter than L320)
2014: 4.4 SDV8 V8 diesel (448DT) introduced as diesel flagship
2014: 5.0 supercharged AJ133 continues from L320 with revisions
2015: SVR performance variant launched with 567hp AJ133S engine
2016: 2.0 Ingenium diesel added to range (late 2016 for 2017 model year)
popularModels: Range Rover Sport SDV6 · Range Rover Sport SDV8 · Range Rover Sport V8 Supercharged · Range Rover Sport SVR · Range Rover Sport Autobiography
knownFor: Aluminium construction improves handling and fuel economy over L320
4.4 SDV8 widely considered the most reliable Sport engine - fewer failure reports than V6 diesel
SVR (567hp) remains a performance benchmark; timing chain tensioner failure still a concern on high-mileage examples
L494 marks the peak of Sport diesel popularity before hybrid models arrived
years.cta: Get quotes for 2013-2017 Range Rover Sport replacement engines
years.ticker: Range Rover Sport SDV6 engine replacement • L494 3.0 diesel crankshaft failure • 4.4 SDV8 recon price • Range Rover Sport SVR engine cost • AJ133 timing chain replacement

Range Rover Sport SVR engine cost

years.preview: L494 LCI: Ingenium Petrol & PHEV Arrive
years.description: The L494 facelift introduced Jaguar Land Rover's Ingenium four-cylinder engines to the Sport for the first time - both the 2.0 Si4 petrol and the 2.0 P400e plug-in hybrid - expanding the range toward lower emissions and company car tax benefits.
keyChanges: 2017: Facelift with revised lights, grille, and interior technology
2017: 2.0 Si4 petrol (PT204, 300hp) joins as entry-level petrol
2017: P400e plug-in hybrid (2.0 petrol + e-motor, 404hp) launched
2018: 3.0 TDV6 phased out; SDV6 remains as sole 3.0 diesel option
2019: 3.0 P400 mild-hybrid petrol (AJ300) introduced (late 2019)
popularModels: Range Rover Sport SDV6 · Range Rover Sport SDV8 · Range Rover Sport Si4 · Range Rover Sport P400e · Range Rover Sport SVR
knownFor: P400e PHEV offered lower Benefit-in-Kind tax for company car drivers
2.0 Si4 petrol is rare in the UK - diesel and V8 petrol dominate replacement enquiries
SDV6 crankshaft failures continue throughout this period despite refinements
PHEV engine replacement requires hybrid-trained technicians and battery inspection

years.ticker: Range Rover Sport SDV6 engine replacement • P400e engine cost • 2.0 Si4 timing chain stretch • 4.4 SDV8 turbo issues

years.preview: L494 Final Years: MHEV Petrol Arrives, SVR Continues
years.description: The final years of the L494 generation saw the introduction of mild-hybrid petrol technology (P400) alongside the continued SDV6 diesel, SDV8, and SVR - before the third-generation L461 arrived in 2022.
keyChanges: 2019: 3.0 P400 mild-hybrid petrol (AJ300, 400hp) introduced
2020: 4.4 SDV8 discontinued due to emissions regulations
2021: SDV6 remains as sole diesel option; crankshaft concerns persist
2022: L494 production ends; L461 third-generation launches

knownFor: P400 mild-hybrid uses 48V electric supercharger for instant throttle response - no turbo lag
SDV6 crankshaft failures remain a constant source of replacement enquiries
SVR ends the L494 generation as the most powerful factory Sport until L461 P530
Late L494 models hold value well; SDV8 cars (discontinued 2020) are increasingly collectable

years.preview: Present - L461 Third Generation: BMW V8, Ingenium Straight-Six MHEV & PHEV
years.description: The third-generation L461 Range Rover Sport launched in 2022 on a new flexible architecture - introducing BMW's N63 4.4 twin-turbo V8 (P530) alongside a full range of Ingenium straight-six mild-hybrid and plug-in hybrid engines.
keyChanges: 2022: All-new L461 platform with modern electrical architecture
2022: AJ300D 3.0 MHEV diesel (D250, D300, D350) replaces SDV6
2022: AJ300 3.0 MHEV petrol (P400) continues from late L494
2022: N63B44 4.4 twin-turbo V8 (P530, 530hp) - BMW-sourced, replaces AJ133
2022: AJ300E 3.0 PHEV (P510e, 510hp) - significantly improved electric range over P400e
popularModels: Range Rover Sport D300 · Range Rover Sport D350 · Range Rover Sport P400 · Range Rover Sport P530 · Range Rover Sport P510e · Range Rover Sport Autobiography
knownFor: BMW-sourced N63 V8 brings hot-vee design - turbochargers inside cylinder banks for response
Straight-six Ingenium engines replace Ford-derived V6 diesels - too early for long-term reliability data
P510e delivers claimed 70km+ electric range - best-in-class for plug-in hybrid SUVs
L461 represents a technology leap; replacement engines are still limited supply but availability is growing

faq.h2: Range Rover Sport Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: How much does a Range Rover Sport engine replacement cost in the UK?
items.answer: Between £3,200 and £9,000 for a reconditioned unit supply-only, plus £900-£1,800 for professional fitting. The 3.0 SDV6 diesel costs £4,250-£6,800, while the 5.0 supercharged V8 (AJ133) ranges £4,700-£6,600.
keyPoints: Reconditioned 3.0 diesel engines: £4,250-£6,800 supply-only
Reconditioned 5.0 V8 petrol (AJ133): £4,700-£6,600 supply-only
Used engines start lower but lack upgraded components
Labour adds 12-16 hours at £90-£140/hour depending on region
items.cta: Compare exact Range Rover Sport engine replacement costs for your variant

items.question: Is it worth replacing the engine on a high-mileage Range Rover Sport?
items.answer: Yes, if the body and chassis are sound. A £4,000-£7,000 reconditioned engine with upgraded components adds £3,000-£5,000 to resale value and delivers 80k-120k more miles - cheaper than buying another used Sport with unknown history.
keyPoints: L320/L494 models worth £8,000-£15,000 justify reconditioned engines
Upgraded crankshafts and timing chains address original design flaws
Reconditioned engines carry 12-month unlimited mileage warranty
Scrapping a sound Sport wastes a vehicle worth more than its repair cost
items.cta: Get a replacement value assessment for your Range Rover Sport

items.question: What causes the 3.0 SDV6 engine to snap its crankshaft?
items.answer: A narrow bearing design inherited from the 2.7 TDV6, combined with oil starvation or extended service intervals, causes bearing spin followed by crankshaft fracture. Once snapped, the engine seizes instantly - no rebuild is possible.
keyPoints: Typically fails between 80,000-140,000 miles without warning
Affects 306DT and 306PS engines in L320 (2010-2013) and L494 (2013-2019)
No preventative fix exists - replacement is the only option
Reconditioned units use upgraded crankshaft and heavy-duty bearings
items.cta: Find a reconditioned 306DT engine with upgraded crankshaft

items.question: How long does a Range Rover Sport engine replacement take from start to finish?
items.answer: 3-7 days total. A reconditioned engine (already built) takes 1-2 days to fit. If your engine is being rebuilt from your old unit, add 5-10 days for strip-down, machining, and assembly before fitting.
keyPoints: Supply-only reconditioned engine: delivered within 2-5 working days
Fitting time: 12-16 hours labour at specialist workshop
Used engine swap: fastest option at 1-2 days total
Full rebuild on your existing engine: 7-14 days typical turnaround
items.cta: Get a firm turnaround quote for your Range Rover Sport engine

items.question: Which Range Rover Sport engine is the most reliable?
items.answer: The 4.4 SDV8 V8 diesel (448DT, 2014-2020) has the fewest failure reports. The 5.0 supercharged V8 is durable when maintained but suffers timing chain tensioner wear at 60k-80k miles. The 3.0 SDV6 has the highest failure rate.
keyPoints: 4.4 SDV8: most reliable diesel, fewer crankshaft and turbo issues
5.0 supercharged V8: strong high-mileage performer with proper maintenance
2.0 Ingenium four-cylinder: avoid - timing chain stretch and turbo failures common
2018-2020 models with supercharged V6 or V8 are best used buys
items.cta: Compare prices for the most reliable Range Rover Sport replacement engines

items.question: What are the symptoms of timing chain failure on a 5.0 V8 Range Rover Sport?
items.answer: Rattling noise on cold start that lasts 1-2 seconds, then fades as oil pressure builds. If ignored, the chain can skip teeth on camshaft sprockets - causing valves to contact pistons and catastrophic engine failure requiring full replacement.
keyPoints: AJ133 engine timing chain tensioner failure typical at 60,000-80,000 miles
Preventative chain replacement costs £1,200-£2,000 at specialist
Full engine replacement after jumped timing: £4,700-£6,600
Upgraded metal tensioners fitted to reconditioned units prevent recurrence
items.cta: Get AJ133 timing chain replacement quotes for your Range Rover Sport

items.question: Can I put a petrol engine in my diesel Range Rover Sport or vice versa?
items.answer: No. The engine mounts, wiring looms, ECU, fuel system, cooling system, and exhaust are completely different between petrol and diesel Sports. Converting is financially ruinous - sell your car and buy the fuel type you want instead.
keyPoints: Petrol ECU cannot communicate with diesel injection system
Fuel tank and lines are incompatible between fuels
Dashboard wiring harness differs across fuel types
DVLA reclassification for fuel type change is complex and rarely approved
items.cta: Find the correct fuel type replacement engine for your Range Rover Sport

items.question: What is the lifespan of a Range Rover Sport diesel engine?
items.answer: 120,000-180,000 miles for a well-maintained 3.0 SDV6 before major failure risk becomes significant. The 4.4 SDV8 regularly exceeds 150,000 miles with proper servicing. The 2.0 Ingenium diesel often fails earlier due to timing chain issues.
keyPoints: Regular oil changes every 10,000-12,000 miles are critical for longevity
SDV6 crankshaft failures most common between 80,000-140,000 miles
SDV8 commonly reaches 150,000+ miles with fewer major failures
Service history is more important than mileage when buying used
items.cta: Check engine availability for your Range Rover Sport's mileage range

items.question: Is the Range Rover Sport PHEV reliable, and what are common engine problems?
items.answer: The P400e plug-in hybrid (2017-2022) adds complexity beyond standard petrol engines. Battery degradation, charging system faults, and rough running when battery depleted are reported. The combustion engine itself shares timing chain concerns with the 2.0 Si4.
keyPoints: High-voltage battery degradation reduces electric range over time
Engine runs rough when battery depleted - relies solely on 2.0 petrol unit
PHEV engine replacement requires hybrid-trained technicians
L461 P510e (2022+) has fewer reported issues but limited long-term data
items.cta: Enquire about PHEV engine availability for your Range Rover Sport

items.question: What should I check before buying a used Range Rover Sport engine?
items.answer: Confirm the EGR cooler has been replaced with updated part LR086253, verify timing chain tensioner has been inspected, request DPF soot load data, and ensure the engine comes from a vehicle with verifiable service history - not just a "low-mileage" claim.
keyPoints: EGR cooler leaks cause hydro-lock - updated cooler is essential on SDV6 units
Request video of engine running from cold to check for timing chain rattle
DPF exceeding 80% soot load indicates short-trip use and potential issues
Always buy with minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty
items.cta: Get vetted Range Rover Sport engine quotes from trusted UK suppliers
